$comment: Single source of truth for the model catalog the cockpit offers, per coding-agent family.
Add a model as soon as it ships — no code change, no version bump: append it here and restart
the stack (systemctl --user restart shellteam-api shellteam-ai-chat). Both the Python control
plane (api/services/model_catalog.py — Fireworks proxy allowlist + OpenCode provider config)
and the Node cockpit (computer/ai-chat/lib/model-catalog.mjs — routing + dropdown) read this file.
match.prefixes drive model->agent routing (a model is also routed by exact membership in an
agent's models[] list). OpenCode models are Fireworks-hosted and proxied through the control
plane; each carries its upstream Fireworks id, so adding one only needs a line here.
'default' (OpenCode) is the model picked when the user selects the OpenCode family.
limit.context = the model's context window in tokens; the cockpit's context-budget meter
(renderContextMeter in public/app.js) shows usage against it. Claude values are the 1M window
Claude Code runs on a subscription (Haiku 4.5 stays 200K); Codex is the GPT-5.x 400K window;
Gemini 3.1 Pro is 2M, 3.6 Flash ~1M. Verified against vendor docs + `agy models` 2026-07-23.

agents.label: Codex
$modelsComment: GPT-5.6 family (released 2026-07-09): three model sizes — Sol (flagship),
Terra (balanced), Luna (cost-efficient). Every cockpit entry pins an explicit
reasoning effort via a model_reasoning_effort `-c` override (`ultra` runs 4
parallel agents; `max` is the top single-agent effort). The four entries below
are the Pareto frontier of the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index (cost vs
which is also the order the dropdown renders (both control planes read this
array top-to-bottom). Context window unchanged from the GPT-5.x 400K.
Verified against Codex CLI 0.144.0: `ultra` and `max` both accepted on these
models. Add lower-effort variants (xhigh/medium) here if a cheaper tier is
wanted — no code change, just restart the stack.

agents.label: Antigravity
agents.$comment: Google Antigravity CLI (`agy`). `cli` = the exact --model display name agy expects (from `agy models`); the cockpit id stays a clean slug. Non-streaming: one JSON envelope per turn, resume via --conversation.
